translations.text: “‘As I live,’ says the Lord Yahweh, ‘surely in the place where the king dwells who made him king, whose oath he despised, and whose covenant he broke, even with him in the middle of Babylon he will die.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Neither shall Pharaoh with his mighty army and great company help him in the war, when they cast up mounds and build forts, to cut off many persons.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he has despised the oath by breaking the covenant; and behold, he had given his hand, and yet has done all these things. He won’t escape.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Therefore thus says the Lord Yahweh: ‘As I live, surely my oath that he has despised, and my covenant that he has broken, I will even bring it on his own head.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will spread my net on him, and he shall be taken in my snare, and I will bring him to Babylon, and will enter into judgment with him there for his trespass that he has trespassed against me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All his fugitives in all his bands shall fall by the sword, and those who remain shall be scattered toward every wind: and you will know that I, Yahweh, have spoken it.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Thus says the Lord Yahweh: ‘I will also take some of the lofty top of the cedar, and will plant it. I will crop off from the topmost of its young twigs a tender one, and I will plant it on a high and lofty mountain.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will plant it in the mountain of the height of Israel; and it will produce boughs, and bear fruit, and be a good cedar. Birds of every kind will dwell in the shade of its branches.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the trees of the field will know that I, Yahweh, have brought down the high tree, have exalted the low tree, have dried up the green tree, and have made the dry tree to flourish. I, Yahweh, have spoken and have done it.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh’s word came to me again, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “What do you mean, that you use this proverb concerning the land of Israel, saying, ‘The fathers have eaten sour grapes, and the children’s teeth are set on edge’?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “As I live,” says the Lord Yahweh, “you shall not use this proverb any more in Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, all souls are mine; as the soul of the father, so also the soul of the son is mine: the soul who sins, he shall die.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “But if a man is just, and does that which is lawful and right,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and has not eaten on the mountains, neither has lifted up his eyes to the idols of the house of Israel, neither has defiled his neighbor’s wife, neither has come near to a woman in her impurity,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and has not wronged any, but has restored to the debtor his pledge, has taken nothing by robbery, has given his bread to the hungry, and has covered the naked with a garment;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: he who has not lent on interest, neither has taken any increase, who has withdrawn his hand from iniquity, has executed true justice between man and man,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: has walked in my statutes, and has kept my ordinances, to deal truly; he is just, he shall surely live,” says the Lord Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If he fathers a son who is a robber who sheds blood, and who does any one of these things,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: or who does not do any of those things, but even has eaten at the mountain shrines, and defiled his neighbor’s wife,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: has wronged the poor and needy, has taken by robbery, has not restored the pledge, and has lifted up his eyes to the idols, has committed abomination,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: has lent on interest, and has taken increase;  shall he then live? He shall not live. He has done all these abominations. He shall surely die. His blood shall be on him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Now, behold, if he fathers a son, who sees all his father’s sins, which he has done, and fears, and does not such like;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: who has not eaten on the mountains, neither has lifted up his eyes to the idols of the house of Israel, has not defiled his neighbor’s wife,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: neither has wronged any, has not taken anything to pledge, neither has taken by robbery, but has given his bread to the hungry, and has covered the naked with a garment;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: who has withdrawn his hand from the poor, who has not received interest nor increase, has executed my ordinances, has walked in my statutes;  he shall not die for the iniquity of his father, he shall surely live.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As for his father, because he cruelly oppressed, robbed his brother, and did that which is not good among his people, behold, he shall die in his iniquity.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Yet you say, ‘Why doesn’t the son bear the iniquity of the father?’ When the son has done that which is lawful and right, and has kept all my statutes, and has done them, he shall surely live.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The soul who sins, he shall die. The son shall not bear the iniquity of the father, neither shall the father bear the iniquity of the son. The righteousness of the righteous shall be on him, and the wickedness of the wicked shall be on him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “But if the wicked turns from all his sins that he has committed, and keeps all my statutes, and does that which is lawful and right, he shall surely live. He shall not die.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: None of his transgressions that he has committed shall be remembered against him. In his righteousness that he has done, he shall live.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Have I any pleasure in the death of the wicked?” says the Lord Yahweh; “and not rather that he should return from his way, and live?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “But when the righteous turns away from his righteousness, and commits iniquity, and does according to all the abominations that the wicked man does, shall he live? None of his righteous deeds that he has done shall be remembered. In his trespass that he has trespassed, and in his sin that he has sinned, in them he shall die.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Yet you say, ‘The way of the Lord is not equal.’ Hear now, house of Israel: Is my way not equal? Aren’t your ways unequal?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the righteous man turns away from his righteousness, and commits iniquity, and dies therein; in his iniquity that he has done shall he die.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Again, when the wicked man turns away from his wickedness that he has committed, and does that which is lawful and right, he shall save his soul alive.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Because he considers, and turns away from all his transgressions that he has committed, he shall surely live. He shall not die.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yet the house of Israel says, ‘The way of the Lord is not fair.’ House of Israel, aren’t my ways fair? Aren’t your ways unfair?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Therefore I will judge you, house of Israel, everyone according to his ways,” says the Lord Yahweh. “Return, and turn yourselves from all your transgressions; so iniquity shall not be your ruin.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Cast away from you all your transgressions, in which you have transgressed; and make yourself a new heart and a new spirit: for why will you die, house of Israel?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For I have no pleasure in the death of him who dies,” says the Lord Yahweh. “Therefore turn yourselves, and live!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Moreover, take up a lamentation for the princes of Israel,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and say, ‘What was your mother? A lioness. She couched among lions, in the middle of the young lions she nourished her cubs.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She brought up one of her cubs. He became a young lion, and he learned to catch the prey. He devoured men.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The nations also heard of him. He was taken in their pit; and they brought him with hooks to the land of Egypt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “‘Now when she saw that she had waited, and her hope was lost, then she took another of her cubs, and made him a young lion.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He went up and down among the lions; he became a young lion, and he learned to catch the prey. He devoured men.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He knew their palaces, and laid waste their cities; and the land was desolate, with its fullness, because of the noise of his roaring.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then the nations set against him on every side from the provinces; and they spread their net over him. He was taken in their pit.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They put him in a cage with hooks, and brought him to the king of Babylon. They brought him into strongholds, that his voice should no more be heard on the mountains of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “‘Your mother was like a vine, in your blood, planted by the waters. It was fruitful and full of branches by reason of many waters.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It had strong rods for the scepters of those who bore rule, and their stature was exalted among the thick boughs, and they were seen in their height with the multitude of their branches.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But it was plucked up in fury, it was cast down to the ground, and the east wind dried up its fruit. Its strong rods were broken off and withered. The fire consumed them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now it is planted in the wilderness, in a dry and thirsty land.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Fire has gone out of the rods of its branches. It has devoured its fruit, so that there is in it no strong rod to be a scepter to rule.’  This is a lamentation, and shall be for a lamentation.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the seventh year, in the fifth month, the tenth day of the month, some of the elders of Israel came to inquire of Yahweh, and sat before me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Son of man, speak to the elders of Israel, and tell them, ‘Thus says the Lord Yahweh: “Is it to inquire of me that you have come? As I live,” says the Lord Yahweh, “I will not be inquired of by you.”’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Will you judge them, son of man, will you judge them? Cause them to know the abominations of their fathers;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and tell them, ‘Thus says the Lord Yahweh: “In the day when I chose Israel, and swore to the offspring  of the house of Jacob, and made myself known to them in the land of Egypt, when I swore to them, saying, ‘I am Yahweh your God;’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: in that day I swore to them, to bring them out of the land of Egypt into a land that I had searched out for them, flowing with milk and honey, which is the glory of all lands.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I said to them, ‘Each of you throw away the abominations of his eyes, and don’t defile yourselves with the idols of Egypt. I am Yahweh your God.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “‘“But they rebelled against me, and would not listen to me. They didn’t all throw away the abominations of their eyes, neither did they forsake the idols of Egypt. Then I said I would pour out my wrath on them, to accomplish my anger against them in the middle of the land of Egypt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But I worked for my name’s sake, that it should not be profaned in the sight of the nations, among which they were, in whose sight I made myself known to them, in bringing them out of the land of Egypt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So I caused them to go out of the land of Egypt, and brought them into the wilderness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I gave them my statutes, and showed them my ordinances, which if a man does, he shall live in them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover also I gave them my Sabbaths, to be a sign between me and them, that they might know that I am Yahweh who sanctifies them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “‘“But the house of Israel rebelled against me in the wilderness. They didn’t walk in my statutes, and they rejected my ordinances, which if a man keeps, he shall live in them. They greatly profaned my Sabbaths. Then I said I would pour out my wrath on them in the wilderness, to consume them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But I worked for my name’s sake, that it should not be profaned in the sight of the nations, in whose sight I brought them out.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover also I swore to them in the wilderness, that I would not bring them into the land which I had given them, flowing with milk and honey, which is the glory of all lands;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: because they rejected my ordinances, and didn’t walk in my statutes, and profaned my Sabbaths; for their heart went after their idols.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Nevertheless my eye spared them, and I didn’t destroy them, neither did I make a full end of them in the wilderness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I said to their children in the wilderness, ‘Don’t walk in the statutes of your fathers, neither observe their ordinances, nor defile yourselves with their idols.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I am Yahweh your God. Walk in my statutes, and keep my ordinances, and do them;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and make my Sabbaths holy; and they shall be a sign between me and you, that you may know that I am Yahweh your God. ’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “‘“But the children rebelled against me. They didn’t walk in my statutes, neither kept my ordinances to do them, which if a man do, he shall live in them. They profaned my Sabbaths. Then I said I would pour out my wrath on them, to accomplish my anger against them in the wilderness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Nevertheless I withdrew my hand, and worked for my name’s sake, that it should not be profaned in the sight of the nations, in whose sight I brought them out.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover I swore to them in the wilderness, that I would scatter them among the nations, and disperse them through the countries;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: because they had not executed my ordinances, but had rejected my statutes, and had profaned my Sabbaths, and their eyes were after their fathers’ idols.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover also I gave them statutes that were not good, and ordinances in which they should not live;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and I polluted them in their own gifts, in that they caused all that opens the womb to pass through the fire, that I might make them desolate, to the end that they might know that I am Yahweh.”’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Therefore, son of man, speak to the house of Israel, and tell them, ‘Thus says the Lord Yahweh: “In this moreover your fathers have blasphemed me, in that they have committed a trespass against me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For when I had brought them into the land, which I swore to give to them, then they saw every high hill, and every thick tree, and they offered there their sacrifices, and there they presented the provocation of their offering; there also they made their pleasant aroma, and they poured out there their drink offerings.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then I said to them, ‘What does the high place where you go mean?’ So its name is called Bamah  to this day. ”’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Therefore tell the house of Israel, ‘Thus says the Lord Yahweh: “Do you pollute yourselves in the way of your fathers? Do you play the prostitute after their abominations?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When you offer your gifts, when you make your sons to pass through the fire, do you pollute yourselves with all your idols to this day? Shall I be inquired of by you, house of Israel? As I live, says the Lord Yahweh, I will not be inquired of by you!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “‘“That which comes into your mind shall not be at all, in that you say, ‘We will be as the nations, as the families of the countries, to serve wood and stone.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As I live, says the Lord Yahweh, surely with a mighty hand, and with an outstretched arm, and with wrath poured out, I will be king over you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will bring you out from the peoples, and will gather you out of the countries in which you are scattered, with a mighty hand, and with an outstretched arm, and with wrath poured out.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will bring you into the wilderness of the peoples, and there I will enter into judgment with you face to face.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Like as I entered into judgment with your fathers in the wilderness of the land of Egypt, so will I enter into judgment with you,” says the Lord Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “I will cause you to pass under the rod, and I will bring you into the bond of the covenant.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will purge out from among you the rebels, and those who disobey against me. I will bring them out of the land where they live, but they shall not enter into the land of Israel; and you shall know that I am Yahweh.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “‘As for you, house of Israel, thus says the Lord Yahweh: “Go, everyone serve his idols, and hereafter also, if you will not listen to me; but you shall no more profane my holy name with your gifts, and with your idols.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For in my holy mountain, in the mountain of the height of Israel,” says the Lord Yahweh, “there shall all the house of Israel, all of them, serve me in the land. There I will accept them, and there I will require your offerings and the first fruits of your offerings, with all your holy things.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As a pleasant aroma I will accept you, when I bring you out from the peoples, and gather you out of the countries in which you have been scattered; and I will be sanctified in you in the sight of the nations.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall know that I am Yahweh, when I bring you into the land of Israel, into the country which I swore to give to your fathers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There you shall remember your ways, and all your doings, in which you have polluted yourselves; and you shall loathe yourselves in your own sight for all your evils that you have committed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall know that I am Yahweh, when I have dealt with you for my name’s sake, not according to your evil ways, nor according to your corrupt doings, you house of Israel,” says the Lord Yahweh.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Son of man, set your face toward the south, and preach toward the south, and prophesy against the forest of the field in the South;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and tell the forest of the South, ‘Hear Yahweh’s word: Thus says the Lord Yahweh, “Behold, I will kindle a fire in you, and it shall devour every green tree in you, and every dry tree: the flaming flame shall not be quenched, and all faces from the south to the north shall be burned thereby.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All flesh shall see that I, Yahweh, have kindled it; it shall not be quenched.”’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then I said, “Ah Lord Yahweh! they say of me, ‘Isn’t he a speaker of parables?’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Son of man, set your face toward Jerusalem, and preach toward the sanctuaries, and prophesy against the land of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Tell the land of Israel, ‘Yahweh says: “Behold, I am against you, and will draw my sword out of its sheath, and will cut off from you the righteous and the wicked.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Seeing then that I will cut off from you the righteous and the wicked, therefore my sword will go out of its sheath against all flesh from the south to the north.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All flesh will know that I, Yahweh, have drawn my sword out of its sheath. It will not return any more.”’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Sigh therefore, you son of man. You shall sigh before their eyes with a broken heart  and with bitterness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It shall be, when they ask you, ‘Why do you sigh?’ that you shall say, ‘Because of the news, for it comes! Every heart will melt, all hands will be feeble, every spirit will faint, and all knees will be weak as water. Behold, it comes, and it shall be done, says the Lord Yahweh.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Son of man, prophesy, and say, ‘Yahweh says: “A sword! A sword! It is sharpened, and also polished.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It is sharpened that it may make a slaughter. It is polished that it may be as lightning. Shall we then make mirth? The rod of my son condemns every tree.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It is given to be polished, that it may be handled. The sword is sharpened. Yes, it is polished, to give it into the hand of the killer.”’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Cry and wail, son of man; for it is on my people. It is on all the princes of Israel. They are delivered over to the sword with my people. Therefore beat your thigh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “For there is a trial. What if even the rod that condemns will be no more?” says the Lord Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You therefore, son of man, prophesy, and strike your hands together. Let the sword be doubled the third time, the sword of the fatally wounded. It is the sword of the great one who is fatally wounded, which enters into their rooms.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I have set the threatening sword against all their gates, that their heart may melt, and their stumblings be multiplied. Ah! It is made as lightning. It is pointed for slaughter.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Gather yourselves together. Go to the right. Set yourselves in array. Go to the left, wherever your face is set.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will also strike my hands together, and I will cause my wrath to rest. I, Yahweh, have spoken it.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh’s word came to me again, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Also, you son of man, appoint two ways, that the sword of the king of Babylon may come. They both shall come out of one land, and mark out a place; mark it out at the head of the way to the city.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall appoint a way for the sword to come to Rabbah of the children of Ammon, and to Judah in Jerusalem the fortified.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the king of Babylon stood at the parting of the way, at the head of the two ways, to use divination. He shook the arrows back and forth. He consulted the teraphim.  He looked in the liver.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In his right hand was the lot for Jerusalem, to set battering rams, to open the mouth in the slaughter, to lift up the voice with shouting, to set battering rams against the gates, to cast up mounds, to build forts.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It shall be to them as a false divination in their sight, who have sworn oaths to them; but he brings iniquity to memory, that they may be taken.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Therefore thus says the Lord Yahweh: ‘Because you have made your iniquity to be remembered, in that your transgressions are uncovered, so that in all your doings your sins appear; because you have come to memory, you shall be taken with the hand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “‘You, deadly wounded wicked one, the prince of Israel, whose day has come, in the time of the iniquity of the end,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: thus says the Lord Yahweh: “Remove the turban, and take off the crown. This will not be as it was. Exalt that which is low, and abase that which is high.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will overturn, overturn, overturn it. This also shall be no more, until he comes whose right it is; and I will give it.”’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You, son of man, prophesy, and say, ‘Thus says the Lord Yahweh concerning the children of Ammon, and concerning their reproach: “A sword! A sword is drawn! It is polished for the slaughter, to cause it to devour, that it may be as lightning;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: while they see for you false visions, while they divine lies to you, to lay you on the necks of the wicked who are deadly wounded, whose day has come in the time of the iniquity of the end.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Cause it to return into its sheath. In the place where you were created, in the land of your birth, will I judge you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will pour out my indignation on you; I will blow on you with the fire of my wrath; and I will deliver you into the hand of brutish men, skillful to destroy.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You will be for fuel to the fire. Your blood will be in the middle of the land. You shall be remembered no more; for I, Yahweh, have spoken it.”’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You, son of man, will you judge? Will you judge the bloody city? Then cause her to know all her abominations.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall say, ‘Thus says the Lord Yahweh: “A city that sheds blood within herself, that her time may come, and that makes idols against herself to defile her!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You have become guilty in your blood that you have shed, and are defiled in your idols which you have made! You have caused your days to draw near, and have come even to your years. Therefore I have made you a reproach to the nations, and a mocking to all the countries.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Those who are near, and those who are far from you, will mock you, you infamous one, full of tumult.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “‘“Behold, the princes of Israel, everyone according to his power, have been in you to shed blood.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In you have they treated father and mother with contempt.  Among you they have oppressed the foreigner. In you they have wronged the fatherless and the widow.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You have despised my holy things, and have profaned my Sabbaths.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Slanderous men have been in you to shed blood. In you they have eaten on the mountains. They have committed lewdness among you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In you have they uncovered their fathers’ nakedness. In you have they humbled her who was unclean in her impurity.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: One has committed abomination with his neighbor’s wife; and another has lewdly defiled his daughter-in-law. Another in you has humbled his sister, his father’s daughter.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In you have they taken bribes to shed blood. You have taken interest and increase, and you have greedily gained of your neighbors by oppression, and have forgotten me,” says the Lord Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “‘“Behold, therefore I have struck my hand at your dishonest gain which you have made, and at your blood which has been within you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Can your heart endure, or can your hands be strong, in the days that I shall deal with you? I, Yahweh, have spoken it, and will do it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will scatter you among the nations, and disperse you through the countries. I will consume your filthiness out of you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You will be profaned in yourself, in the sight of the nations; and you shall know that I am Yahweh.”’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Son of man, the house of Israel has become dross to me. All of them are brass, tin, iron, and lead in the middle of the furnace. They are the dross of silver.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore thus says the Lord Yahweh: ‘Because you have all become dross, therefore, behold, I will gather you into the middle of Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As they gather silver, brass, iron, lead, and tin into the middle of the furnace, to blow the fire on it, to melt it; so will I gather you in my anger and in my wrath, and I will lay you there, and melt you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yes, I will gather you, and blow on you with the fire of my wrath, and you will be melted in the middle of it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As silver is melted in the middle of the furnace, so you will be melted in the middle of it; and you will know that I, Yahweh, have poured out my wrath on you.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Son of man, tell her, ‘You are a land that is not cleansed, nor rained on in the day of indignation.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There is a conspiracy of her prophets within it, like a roaring lion ravening the prey. They have devoured souls. They take treasure and precious things. They have made her widows many within it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Her priests have done violence to my law, and have profaned my holy things. They have made no distinction between the holy and the common, neither have they caused men to discern between the unclean and the clean, and have hidden their eyes from my Sabbaths, and I am profaned among them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Her princes within it are like wolves ravening the prey, to shed blood, and to destroy souls, that they may get dishonest gain.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Her prophets have plastered for them with whitewash, seeing false visions, and divining lies to them, saying, ‘Thus says the Lord Yahweh,’ when Yahweh has not spoken.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The people of the land have used oppression, and exercised robbery. Yes, they have troubled the poor and needy, and have oppressed the foreigner wrongfully.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “I sought for a man among them, who should build up the wall, and stand in the gap before me for the land, that I should not destroy it; but I found no one.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore I have poured out my indignation on them. I have consumed them with the fire of my wrath. I have brought their own way on their heads,” says the Lord Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh’s word came again to me, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Son of man, there were two women, the daughters of one mother.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They played the prostitute in Egypt. They played the prostitute in their youth. Their breasts were fondled there, and their youthful nipples were caressed there.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Their names were Oholah the elder, and Oholibah her sister. They became mine, and they bore sons and daughters. As for their names, Samaria is Oholah, and Jerusalem Oholibah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Oholah played the prostitute when she was mine. She doted on her lovers, on the Assyrians her neighbors,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: who were clothed with blue, governors and rulers, all of them desirable young men, horsemen riding on horses.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She gave herself as a prostitute to them, the choicest men of Assyria all of them. She defiled herself with the idols of whoever she lusted after.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She hasn’t left her prostitution since leaving Egypt; for in her youth they lay with her. They carressed her youthful nipples; and they poured out their prostitution on her.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Therefore I delivered her into the hand of her lovers, into the hand of the Assyrians, on whom she doted.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: These uncovered her nakedness. They took her sons and her daughters; and they killed her with the sword. She became a byword among women; for they executed judgments on her.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Her sister Oholibah saw this, yet she was more corrupt in her lusting than she, and in her prostitution which was more depraved than the prostitution of her sister.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She lusted after the Assyrians, governors and rulers, her neighbors, clothed most gorgeously, horsemen riding on horses, all of them desirable young men.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I saw that she was defiled. They both went the same way.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “She increased her prostitution; for she saw men portrayed on the wall, the images of the Chaldeans portrayed with vermilion,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: dressed with girdles on their waists, with flowing turbans on their heads, all of them looking like princes, after the likeness of the Babylonians in Chaldea, the land of their birth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As soon as she saw them she lusted after them, and sent messengers to them into Chaldea.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Babylonians came to her into the bed of love, and they defiled her with their prostitution. She was polluted with them, and her soul was alienated from them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So she uncovered her prostitution, and uncovered her nakedness. Then my soul was alienated from her, just like my soul was alienated from her sister.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yet she multiplied her prostitution, remembering the days of her youth, in which she had played the prostitute in the land of Egypt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She lusted after their paramours, whose flesh is as the flesh of donkeys, and whose issue is like the issue of horses.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thus you called to memory the lewdness of your youth, in the caressing of your nipples by the Egyptians because of your youthful breasts.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Therefore, Oholibah, thus says the Lord Yahweh: ‘Behold, I will raise up your lovers against you, from whom your soul is alienated, and I will bring them against you on every side:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the Babylonians and all the Chaldeans, Pekod, Shoa, Koa, and all the Assyrians with them; desirable young men, governors and rulers all of them, princes and men of renown, all of them riding on horses.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They will come against you with weapons, chariots, and wagons, and with a company of peoples. They will set themselves against you with buckler, shield, and helmet all around. I will commit the judgment to them, and they will judge you according to their judgments.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will set my jealousy against you, and they will deal with you in fury; they will take away your nose and your ears; and your remnant will fall by the sword. They will take your sons and your daughters; and your residue will be devoured by the fire.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They will also strip you of your clothes, and take away your beautiful jewels.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thus I will make your lewdness to cease from you, and remove your prostitution from the land of Egypt; so that you will not lift up your eyes to them, nor remember Egypt any more.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “For thus says the Lord Yahweh: ‘Behold, I will deliver you into the hand of them whom you hate, into the hand of them from whom your soul is alienated.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They will deal with you in hatred, and will take away all your labor, and shall leave you naked and bare. The nakedness of your prostitution will be uncovered, both your lewdness and your prostitution.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: These things will be done to you, because you have played the prostitute after the nations, and because you are polluted with their idols.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You have walked in the way of your sister; therefore will I give her cup into your hand.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Thus says the Lord Yahweh: ‘You will drink of your sister’s cup, which is deep and large. You will be ridiculed and held in derision. It contains much.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You will be filled with drunkenness and sorrow, with the cup of astonishment and desolation, with the cup of your sister Samaria.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You will even drink it and drain it out. You will gnaw the broken pieces of it, and will tear your breasts;  for I have spoken it,’ says the Lord Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Therefore thus says the Lord Yahweh: ‘Because you have forgotten me, and cast me behind your back, therefore you also bear your lewdness and your prostitution.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said moreover to me: “Son of man, will you judge Oholah and Oholibah? Then declare to them their abominations.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For they have committed adultery, and blood is in their hands. They have committed adultery with their idols. They have also caused their sons, whom they bore to me, to pass through the fire to them to be devoured.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover this they have done to me: they have defiled my sanctuary in the same day, and have profaned my Sabbaths.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For when they had slain their children to their idols, then they came the same day into my sanctuary to profane it; and behold, thus have they done in the middle of my house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Furthermore you sisters have sent for men who come from far away, to whom a messenger was sent, and behold, they came; for whom you washed yourself, painted your eyes, decorated yourself with ornaments,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and sat on a stately bed, with a table prepared before it, whereupon you set my incense and my oil.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “The voice of a multitude being at ease was with her. With men of the common sort were brought drunkards from the wilderness; and they put bracelets on their hands, and beautiful crowns on their heads.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then I said of her who was old in adulteries, ‘Now they will play the prostitute with her, and she with them.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They went in to her, as they go in to a prostitute. So they went in to Oholah and to Oholibah, the lewd women.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Righteous men will judge them with the judgment of adulteresses and with the judgment of women who shed blood; because they are adulteresses, and blood is in their hands.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “For thus says the Lord Yahweh: ‘I will bring up a company against them, and will give them to be tossed back and forth and robbed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The company will stone them with stones, and dispatch them with their swords. They will kill their sons and their daughters, and burn up their houses with fire.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “‘Thus I will cause lewdness to cease out of the land, that all women may be taught not to be lewd like you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They shall recompense your lewdness on you, and you will bear the sins of your idols; and you shall know that I am the Lord Yahweh.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Again, in the ninth year, in the tenth month, in the tenth day of the month, Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Son of man, write the name of the day, this same day. The king of Babylon drew close to Jerusalem this same day.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Utter a parable to the rebellious house, and tell them, Thus says the Lord Yahweh, Set on the cauldron, set it on, and also pour water into it:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: gather its pieces into it, even every good piece, the thigh, and the shoulder; fill it with the choice bones.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Take the choice of the flock, and also a pile of wood for the bones under the cauldron; make it boil well; yes, let its bones be boiled within it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore thus says the Lord Yahweh: Woe to the bloody city, to the cauldron whose rust is therein, and whose rust is not gone out of it! take out of it piece after piece; No lot is fallen on it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For her blood is in the middle of her; she set it on the bare rock; she didn’t pour it on the ground, to cover it with dust.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: That it may cause wrath to come up to take vengeance, I have set her blood on the bare rock, that it should not be covered.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore thus says the Lord Yahweh: Woe to the bloody city! I also will make the pile great.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Heap on the wood, make the fire hot, boil the meat well, and make the broth thick, and let the bones be burned.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then set it empty on its coals, that it may be hot, and its brass may burn, and that its filthiness may be molten in it, that its rust may be consumed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She is weary with toil; yet her great rust, rust by fire, doesn’t leave her.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In your filthiness is lewdness: because I have cleansed you and you weren’t cleansed, you shall not be cleansed from your filthiness any more, until I have caused my wrath toward you to rest.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I, Yahweh, have spoken it: it shall happen, and I will do it: I will not go back, neither will I spare, neither will I repent; according to your ways, and according to your doings, shall they judge you, says the Lord Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Also Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Son of man, behold, I will take away from you the desire of your eyes with a stroke: yet you shall neither mourn nor weep, neither shall your tears run down.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Sigh, but not aloud, make no mourning for the dead; bind your headdress on you, and put your shoes on your feet, and don’t cover your lips, and don’t eat men’s bread.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So I spoke to the people in the morning; and at evening my wife died; and I did in the morning as I was commanded.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The people said to me, Won’t you tell us what these things are to us, that you do so?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then I said to them, Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Speak to the house of Israel, Thus says the Lord Yahweh: Behold, I will profane my sanctuary, the pride of your power, the desire of your eyes, and that which your soul pities; and your sons and your daughters whom you have left behind shall fall by the sword.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall do as I have done: you shall not cover your lips, nor eat the bread of men.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Your tires shall be on your heads, and your shoes on your feet: you shall not mourn nor weep; but you shall pine away in your iniquities, and moan one toward another.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thus Ezekiel shall be a sign to you; according to all that he has done, you will do. When this comes, then you will know that I am the Lord Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You, son of man, shall it not be in the day when I take from them their strength, the joy of their glory, the desire of their eyes, and that whereupon they set their heart, their sons and their daughters,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: that in that day he who escapes shall come to you, to cause you to hear it with your ears?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In that day your mouth will be opened to him who has escaped, and you shall speak, and be no more mute: so you will be a sign to them; and they shall know that I am Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Son of man, set your face toward the children of Ammon, and prophesy against them:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and tell the children of Ammon, Hear the word of the Lord Yahweh: Thus says the Lord Yahweh, Because you said, Aha, against my sanctuary, when it was profaned; and against the land of Israel, when it was made desolate; and against the house of Judah, when they went into captivity:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: therefore, behold, I will deliver you to the children of the east for a possession, and they shall set their encampments in you, and make their dwellings in you; they shall eat your fruit, and they shall drink your milk.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will make Rabbah a stable for camels, and the children of Ammon a resting place for flocks: and you shall know that I am Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For thus says the Lord Yahweh: Because you have clapped your hands, and stamped with the feet, and rejoiced with all the despite of your soul against the land of Israel;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: therefore, behold, I have stretched out my hand on you, and will deliver you for a plunder to the nations; and I will cut you off from the peoples, and I will cause you to perish out of the countries: I will destroy you; and you shall know that I am Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thus says the Lord Yahweh: Because Moab and Seir say, Behold, the house of Judah is like all the nations;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: therefore, behold, I will open the side of Moab from the cities, from his cities which are on his frontiers, the glory of the country, Beth Jeshimoth, Baal Meon, and Kiriathaim,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: to the children of the east, to go against the children of Ammon; and I will give them for a possession, that the children of Ammon may not be remembered among the nations.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and I will execute judgments on Moab; and they shall know that I am Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thus says the Lord Yahweh: Because Edom has dealt against the house of Judah by taking vengeance, and has greatly offended, and revenged himself on them;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: therefore thus says the Lord Yahweh, I will stretch out my hand on Edom, and will cut off man and animal from it; and I will make it desolate from Teman; even to Dedan shall they fall by the sword.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will lay my vengeance on Edom by the hand of my people Israel; and they shall do in Edom according to my anger and according to my wrath; and they shall know my vengeance, says the Lord Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thus says the Lord Yahweh: Because the Philistines have dealt by revenge, and have taken vengeance with despite of soul to destroy with perpetual hostility;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: therefore thus says the Lord Yahweh, Behold, I will stretch out my hand on the Philistines, and I will cut off the Cherethites, and destroy the remnant of the sea coast.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will execute great vengeance on them with wrathful rebukes; and they shall know that I am Yahweh, when I shall lay my vengeance on them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the eleventh year, in the first of the month, Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Son of man, because Tyre has said against Jerusalem, Aha, she is broken: the gate of the peoples; she is turned to me; I shall be replenished, now that she is laid waste:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: therefore thus says the Lord Yahweh, Behold, I am against you, Tyre, and will cause many nations to come up against you, as the sea causes its waves to come up.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They shall destroy the walls of Tyre, and break down her towers: I will also scrape her dust from her, and make her a bare rock.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She shall be a place for the spreading of nets in the middle of the sea; for I have spoken it, says the Lord Yahweh; and she shall become a plunder to the nations.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Her daughters who are in the field shall be slain with the sword: and they shall know that I am Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For thus says the Lord Yahweh: Behold, I will bring on Tyre Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon, king of kings, from the north, with horses, and with chariots, and with horsemen, and a company, and many people.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He shall kill your daughters in the field with the sword; and he shall make forts against you, and cast up a mound against you, and raise up the buckler against you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He shall set his battering engines against your walls, and with his axes he shall break down your towers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: By reason of the abundance of his horses their dust shall cover you: your walls shall shake at the noise of the horsemen, and of the wagons, and of the chariots, when he shall enter into your gates, as men enter into a city in which is made a breach.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: With the hoofs of his horses shall he tread down all your streets; he shall kill your people with the sword; and the pillars of your strength shall go down to the ground.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They shall make a plunder of your riches, and make a prey of your merchandise; and they shall break down your walls, and destroy your pleasant houses; and they shall lay your stones and your timber and your dust in the middle of the waters.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will cause the noise of your songs to cease; and the sound of your harps shall be no more heard.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will make you a bare rock; you shall be a place for the spreading of nets; you shall be built no more: for I Yahweh have spoken it, says the Lord Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thus says the Lord Yahweh to Tyre: shall not the islands shake at the sound of your fall, when the wounded groan, when the slaughter is made within you?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then all the princes of the sea shall come down from their thrones, and lay aside their robes, and strip off their embroidered garments: they shall clothe themselves with trembling; they shall sit on the ground, and shall tremble every moment, and be astonished at you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They shall take up a lamentation over you, and tell you, How you are destroyed, who were inhabited by seafaring men, the renowned city, who was strong in the sea, she and her inhabitants, who caused their terror to be on all who lived there!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now shall the islands tremble in the day of your fall; yes, the islands that are in the sea shall be dismayed at your departure.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For thus says the Lord Yahweh: When I shall make you a desolate city, like the cities that are not inhabited; when I shall bring up the deep on you, and the great waters shall cover you;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: then I will bring you down with those who descend into the pit, to the people of old time, and will make you to dwell in the lower parts of the earth, in the places that are desolate of old, with those who go down to the pit, that you be not inhabited; and I will set glory in the land of the living:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will make you a terror, and you shall no more have any being; though you are sought for, yet you will never be found again, says the Lord Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh’s word came again to me, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You, son of man, take up a lamentation over Tyre;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and tell Tyre, you who dwell at the entry of the sea, who are the merchant of the peoples to many islands, thus says the Lord Yahweh: You, Tyre, have said, I am perfect in beauty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Your borders are in the heart of the seas; your builders have perfected your beauty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They have made all your planks of cypress trees from Senir; they have taken a cedar from Lebanon to make a mast for you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Of the oaks of Bashan have they made your oars; they have made your benches of ivory inlaid in boxwood, from the islands of Kittim.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Your sail was of fine linen with embroidered work from Egypt, that it might be to you for a banner; blue and purple from the islands of Elishah was your awning.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The inhabitants of Sidon and Arvad were your rowers: your wise men, Tyre, were in you, they were your pilots.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The old men of Gebal and its wise men were your repairers of ship seams in you: all the ships of the sea with their mariners were in you to deal in your merchandise.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Persia and Lud and Put were in your army, your men of war: they hanged the shield and helmet in you; they showed your beauty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The men of Arvad with your army were on your walls all around, and valorous men were in your towers; they hung their shields on your walls all around; they have perfected your beauty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Tarshish was your merchant by reason of the multitude of all kinds of riches; with silver, iron, tin, and lead, they traded for your wares.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Javan, Tubal, and Meshech, they were your traffickers; they traded the persons of men and vessels of brass for your merchandise.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They of the house of Togarmah traded for your wares with horses and war horses and mules.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The men of Dedan were your traffickers; many islands were the market of your hand: they brought you in exchange horns of ivory and ebony.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Syria was your merchant by reason of the multitude of your handiworks: they traded for your wares with emeralds, purple, and embroidered work, and fine linen, and coral, and rubies.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Judah, and the land of Israel, they were your traffickers: they traded for your merchandise wheat of Minnith, and confections, and honey, and oil, and balm.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Damascus was your merchant for the multitude of your handiworks, by reason of the multitude of all kinds of riches, with the wine of Helbon, and white wool.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Vedan and Javan traded with yarn for your wares: bright iron, cassia, and calamus, were among your merchandise.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Dedan was your trafficker in precious cloths for riding.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Arabia, and all the princes of Kedar, they were the merchants of your hand; in lambs, and rams, and goats, in these were they your merchants.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The traffickers of Sheba and Raamah, they were your traffickers; they traded for your wares with the chief of all spices, and with all precious stones, and gold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Haran and Canneh and Eden, the traffickers of Sheba, Asshur and Chilmad, were your traffickers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: These were your traffickers in choice wares, in wrappings of blue and embroidered work, and in chests of rich clothing, bound with cords and made of cedar, among your merchandise.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The ships of Tarshish were your caravans for your merchandise: and you were replenished, and made very glorious in the heart of the seas.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Your rowers have brought you into great waters: the east wind has broken you in the heart of the seas.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Your riches, and your wares, your merchandise, your mariners, and your pilots, your repairers of ship seams, and the dealers in your merchandise, and all your men of war, who are in you, with all your company which is among you, shall fall into the heart of the seas in the day of your ruin.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: At the sound of the cry of your pilots the suburbs shall shake.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All who handled the oar, the mariners, and all the pilots of the sea, shall come down from their ships; they shall stand on the land,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and shall cause their voice to be heard over you, and shall cry bitterly, and shall cast up dust on their heads, they shall wallow themselves in the ashes:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and they shall make themselves bald for you, and clothe them with sackcloth, and they shall weep for you in bitterness of soul with bitter mourning.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In their wailing they shall take up a lamentation for you, and lament over you, saying, Who is there like Tyre, like her who is brought to silence in the middle of the sea?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When your wares went out of the seas, you filled many peoples; you enriched the kings of the earth with the multitude of your riches and of your merchandise.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the time that you were broken by the seas in the depths of the waters, your merchandise and all your company fell within you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the inhabitants of the islands are astonished at you, and their kings are horribly afraid; they are troubled in their face.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The merchants among the peoples hiss at you; you have become a terror, and you will be no more.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh’s word came again to me, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Son of man, tell the prince of Tyre, Thus says the Lord Yahweh: Because your heart is lifted up, and you have said, I am a god, I sit in the seat of God, in the middle of the seas; yet you are man, and not God, though you set your heart as the heart of God —

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: behold, you are wiser than Daniel; there is no secret that is hidden from you;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: by your wisdom and by your understanding you have gotten yourself riches, and have gotten gold and silver into your treasures;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: by your great wisdom and by your traffic you have increased your riches, and your heart is lifted up because of your riches —

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: therefore thus says the Lord Yahweh: Because you have set your heart as the heart of God,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: therefore, behold, I will bring strangers on you, the terrible of the nations; and they shall draw their swords against the beauty of your wisdom, and they shall defile your brightness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They shall bring you down to the pit; and you shall die the death of those who are slain, in the heart of the seas.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Will you yet say before him who kills you, I am God? but you are man, and not God, in the hand of him who wounds you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall die the death of the uncircumcised by the hand of strangers: for I have spoken it, says the Lord Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Son of man, take up a lamentation over the king of Tyre, and tell him, Thus says the Lord Yahweh: You were the seal of full measure, full of wisdom, and perfect in beauty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You were in Eden, the garden of God; every precious stone adorned you: ruby, topaz, emerald, chrysolite, onyx, jasper, sapphire,  turquoise, and beryl. Gold work of tambourines and of pipes was in you. In the day that you were created they were prepared.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You were the anointed cherub who covers: and I set you, so that you were on the holy mountain of God; you have walked up and down in the middle of the stones of fire.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You were perfect in your ways from the day that you were created, until unrighteousness was found in you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: By the abundance of your traffic they filled your insides with violence, and you have sinned: therefore I have cast you as profane out of the mountain of God; and I have destroyed you, covering cherub, from the middle of the stones of fire.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Your heart was lifted up because of your beauty; you have corrupted your wisdom by reason of your brightness: I have cast you to the ground; I have laid you before kings, that they may see you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: By the multitude of your iniquities, in the unrighteousness of your traffic, you have profaned your sanctuaries; therefore I have brought out a fire from the middle of you; it has devoured you, and I have turned you to ashes on the earth in the sight of all those who see you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All those who know you among the peoples shall be astonished at you: you have become a terror, and you shall exist no more.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Son of man, set your face toward Sidon, and prophesy against it,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and say, Thus says the Lord Yahweh: Behold, I am against you, Sidon; and I will be glorified among you; and they shall know that I am Yahweh, when I shall have executed judgments in her, and shall be sanctified in her.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For I will send pestilence into her, and blood into her streets; and the wounded shall fall within her, with the sword on her on every side; and they shall know that I am Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There shall be no more a pricking brier to the house of Israel, nor a hurting thorn of any that are around them, that scorned them; and they shall know that I am the Lord Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thus says the Lord Yahweh: When I shall have gathered the house of Israel from the peoples among whom they are scattered, and shall be sanctified in them in the sight of the nations, then shall they dwell in their own land which I gave to my servant Jacob.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They shall dwell securely therein; yes, they shall build houses, and plant vineyards, and shall dwell securely, when I have executed judgments on all those who do them despite all around them; and they shall know that I am Yahweh their God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the tenth year, in the tenth month, on the twelfth day of the month, Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Son of man, set your face against Pharaoh king of Egypt, and prophesy against him and against all Egypt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Speak and say, ‘Thus says the Lord Yahweh: “Behold, I am against you, Pharaoh king of Egypt, the great monster that lies in the middle of his rivers, that has said, ‘My river is my own, and I have made it for myself.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will put hooks in your jaws, and I will make the fish of your rivers stick to your scales; and I will bring you up out of the middle of your rivers, with all the fish of your rivers which stick to your scales.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I’ll cast you out into the wilderness, you and all the fish of your rivers. You’ll fall on the open field. You won’t be brought together, nor gathered. I have given you for food to the animals of the earth and to the birds of the sky.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the inhabitants of Egypt will know that I am Yahweh, because they have been a staff of reed to the house of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they took hold of you by your hand, you broke, and tore all their shoulders; and when they leaned on you, you broke, and paralyzed all of their thighs.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore thus says the Lord Yahweh: “Behold, I will bring a sword on you, and will cut off man and animal from you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The land of Egypt shall be a desolation and a waste; and they shall know that I am Yahweh. Because he has said, ‘The river is mine, and I have made it;’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: therefore, behold, I am against you, and against your rivers, and I will make the land of Egypt an utter waste and desolation, from the tower of Seveneh even to the border of Ethiopia.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: No foot of man shall pass through it, nor foot of animal shall pass through it, neither shall it be inhabited forty years.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will make the land of Egypt a desolation in the middle of the countries that are desolate; and her cities among the cities that are laid waste shall be a desolation forty years; and I will scatter the Egyptians among the nations, and will disperse them through the countries.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For thus says the Lord Yahweh: “At the end of forty years will I gather the Egyptians from the peoples where they were scattered;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and I will bring back the captivity of Egypt, and will cause them to return into the land of Pathros, into the land of their birth; and they shall be there a base kingdom.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It shall be the base of the kingdoms; neither shall it any more lift itself up above the nations: and I will diminish them, that they shall no more rule over the nations.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It shall be no more the confidence of the house of Israel, bringing iniquity to memory, when they turn to look after them: and they shall know that I am the Lord Yahweh.”’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It came to pass in the seven and twentieth year, in the first month, in the first day of the month, Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Son of man, Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon caused his army to serve a great service against Tyre: every head was made bald, and every shoulder was worn; yet had he no wages, nor his army, from Tyre, for the service that he had served against it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore thus says the Lord Yahweh: Behold, I will give the land of Egypt to Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon; and he shall carry off her multitude, and take her plunder, and take her prey; and it shall be the wages for his army.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I have given him the land of Egypt as his recompense for which he served, because they worked for me, says the Lord Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In that day will I cause a horn to sprout for the house of Israel, and I will give you the opening of the mouth among them; and they shall know that I am Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh’s word came again to me, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Son of man, prophesy, and say, Thus says the Lord Yahweh: Wail, Alas for the day!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the day is near, even the day of Yahweh is near; it shall be a day of clouds, a time of the nations.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A sword shall come on Egypt, and anguish shall be in Ethiopia, when the slain shall fall in Egypt; and they shall take away her multitude, and her foundations shall be broken down.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Ethiopia, and Put, and Lud, and all the mixed people, and Cub, and the children of the land that is allied with them, shall fall with them by the sword.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh says: They also who uphold Egypt shall fall; and the pride of her power shall come down: they shall fall by the sword in it from the tower of Seveneh, says the Lord Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They shall be desolate in the middle of the countries that are desolate; and her cities shall be in the middle of the cities that are wasted.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They shall know that I am Yahweh, when I have set a fire in Egypt, and all her helpers are destroyed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In that day messengers will go out from before me in ships to make the careless Ethiopians afraid; and there shall be anguish on them, as in the day of Egypt; for, behold, it comes.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thus says the Lord Yahweh: I will also make the multitude of Egypt to cease, by the hand of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He and his people with him, the terrible of the nations, shall be brought in to destroy the land; and they shall draw their swords against Egypt, and fill the land with the slain.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will make the rivers dry, and will sell the land into the hand of evil men; and I will make the land desolate, and all that is therein, by the hand of strangers: I, Yahweh, have spoken it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thus says the Lord Yahweh: I will also destroy the idols, and I will cause the images to cease from Memphis; and there shall be no more a prince from the land of Egypt: and I will put a fear in the land of Egypt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will make Pathros desolate, and will set a fire in Zoan, and will execute judgments on No.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will pour my wrath on Sin, the stronghold of Egypt; and I will cut off the multitude of No.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will set a fire in Egypt: Sin shall be in great anguish, and No shall be broken up; and Memphis shall have adversaries in the daytime.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The young men of Aven and of Pibeseth shall fall by the sword; and they shall go into captivity.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: At Tehaphnehes also the day shall withdraw itself, when I shall break there the yokes of Egypt, and the pride of her power shall cease in her: as for her, a cloud shall cover her, and her daughters shall go into captivity.
