translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thus will I execute judgments on Egypt; and they shall know that I am Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the eleventh year, in the first month, in the seventh day of the month, Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Son of man, I have broken the arm of Pharaoh king of Egypt; and behold, it has not been bound up, to apply medicines, to put a bandage to bind it, that it be strong to hold the sword.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore thus says the Lord Yahweh: Behold, I am against Pharaoh king of Egypt, and will break his arms, the strong arm, and that which was broken; and I will cause the sword to fall out of his hand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will scatter the Egyptians among the nations, and will disperse them through the countries.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will strengthen the arms of the king of Babylon, and put my sword in his hand: but I will break the arms of Pharaoh, and he shall groan before him with the groaning of a mortally wounded man.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will hold up the arms of the king of Babylon; and the arms of Pharaoh shall fall down; and they shall know that I am Yahweh, when I shall put my sword into the hand of the king of Babylon, and he shall stretch it out on the land of Egypt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will scatter the Egyptians among the nations, and disperse them through the countries; and they shall know that I am Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the eleventh year, in the third month, in the first day of the month, Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Son of man, tell Pharaoh king of Egypt, and to his multitude: Whom are you like in your greatness?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, the Assyrian was a cedar in Lebanon with beautiful branches, and with a forest-like shade, and of high stature; and its top was among the thick boughs.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The waters nourished it, the deep made it to grow: its rivers ran all around its plantation; and it sent out its channels to all the trees of the field.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore its stature was exalted above all the trees of the field; and its boughs were multiplied, and its branches became long by reason of many waters, when it spread them out.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the birds of the sky made their nests in its boughs; and under its branches all the animals of the field gave birth to their young; and all great nations lived under its shadow.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thus was it beautiful in its greatness, in the length of its branches; for its root was by many waters.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The cedars in the garden of God could not hide it; the cypress trees were not like its boughs, and the pine trees were not as its branches; nor was any tree in the garden of God like it in its beauty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I made it beautiful by the multitude of its branches, so that all the trees of Eden, that were in the garden of God, envied it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore thus said the Lord Yahweh: Because you are exalted in stature, and he has set his top among the thick boughs, and his heart is lifted up in his height;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will even deliver him into the hand of the mighty one of the nations; he shall surely deal with him; I have driven him out for his wickedness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Strangers, the terrible of the nations, have cut him off, and have left him: on the mountains and in all the valleys his branches are fallen, and his boughs are broken by all the watercourses of the land; and all the peoples of the earth are gone down from his shadow, and have left him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: On his ruin all the birds of the sky shall dwell, and all the animals of the field shall be on his branches;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: to the end that none of all the trees by the waters exalt themselves in their stature, neither set their top among the thick boughs, nor that their mighty ones stand up on their height, even all who drink water: for they are all delivered to death, to the lower parts of the earth, among the children of men, with those who go down to the pit.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thus says the Lord Yahweh: In the day when he went down to Sheol  I caused a mourning: I covered the deep for him, and I restrained its rivers; and the great waters were stayed; and I caused Lebanon to mourn for him, and all the trees of the field fainted for him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I made the nations to shake at the sound of his fall, when I cast him down to Sheol  with those who descend into the pit; and all the trees of Eden, the choice and best of Lebanon, all that drink water, were comforted in the lower parts of the earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They also went down into Sheol with him to those who are slain by the sword; yes, those who were his arm, who lived under his shadow in the middle of the nations.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: To whom are you thus like in glory and in greatness among the trees of Eden? yet you will be brought down with the trees of Eden to the lower parts of the earth: you shall lie in the middle of the uncircumcised, with those who are slain by the sword. This is Pharaoh and all his multitude, says the Lord Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the twelfth year, in the twelfth month, in the first day of the month, Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Son of man, take up a lamentation over Pharaoh king of Egypt, and tell him, You were likened to a young lion of the nations: yet you are as a monster in the seas; and you broke out with your rivers, and troubled the waters with your feet, and fouled their rivers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thus says the Lord Yahweh: I will spread out my net on you with a company of many peoples; and they shall bring you up in my net.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will leave you on the land, I will cast you out on the open field, and will cause all the birds of the sky to settle on you, and I will satisfy the animals of the whole earth with you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will lay your flesh on the mountains, and fill the valleys with your height.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will also water with your blood the land in which you swim, even to the mountains; and the watercourses shall be full of you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When I shall extinguish you, I will cover the heavens, and make its stars dark; I will cover the sun with a cloud, and the moon shall not give its light.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will make all the bright lights of the sky dark over you, and set darkness on your land, says the Lord Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will also trouble the hearts of many peoples, when I shall bring your destruction among the nations, into the countries which you have not known.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yes, I will make many peoples amazed at you, and their kings shall be horribly afraid for you, when I shall brandish my sword before them; and they shall tremble at every moment, every man for his own life, in the day of your fall.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For thus says the Lord Yahweh: The sword of the king of Babylon shall come on you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: By the swords of the mighty will I cause your multitude to fall; they are all the ruthless of the nations: and they shall bring to nothing the pride of Egypt, and all its multitude shall be destroyed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will destroy also all its animals from beside many waters; neither shall the foot of man trouble them any more, nor the hoofs of animals trouble them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then will I make their waters clear, and cause their rivers to run like oil, says the Lord Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When I shall make the land of Egypt desolate and waste, a land destitute of that of which it was full, when I shall strike all those who dwell therein, then shall they know that I am Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: This is the lamentation with which they shall lament; the daughters of the nations shall lament therewith; over Egypt, and over all her multitude, shall they lament therewith, says the Lord Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Also in the twelfth year, in the fifteenth day of the month, Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Son of man, wail for the multitude of Egypt, and cast them down, even her, and the daughters of the famous nations, to the lower parts of the earth, with those who go down into the pit.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whom do you pass in beauty? Go down, and be laid with the uncircumcised.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They shall fall among those who are slain by the sword: she is delivered to the sword; draw her away and all her multitudes.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The strong among the mighty shall speak to him out of the middle of Sheol  with those who help him: they are gone down, they lie still, even the uncircumcised, slain by the sword.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Asshur is there and all her company; her graves are all around her; all of them slain, fallen by the sword;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: whose graves are set in the uttermost parts of the pit, and her company is around her grave; all of them slain, fallen by the sword, who caused terror in the land of the living.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There is Elam and all her multitude around her grave; all of them slain, fallen by the sword, who are gone down uncircumcised into the lower parts of the earth, who caused their terror in the land of the living, and have borne their shame with those who go down to the pit.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They have set her a bed among the slain with all her multitude; her graves are around her; all of them uncircumcised, slain by the sword; for their terror was caused in the land of the living, and they have borne their shame with those who go down to the pit: he is put among those who are slain.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There is Meshech, Tubal, and all their multitude; their graves are around them; all of them uncircumcised, slain by the sword; for they caused their terror in the land of the living.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They shall not lie with the mighty who are fallen of the uncircumcised, who have gone down to Sheol with their weapons of war, and have laid their swords under their heads, and their iniquities are on their bones; for they were the terror of the mighty in the land of the living.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But you shall be broken among the uncircumcised, and shall lie with those who are slain by the sword.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There is Edom, her kings and all her princes, who in their might are laid with those who are slain by the sword: they shall lie with the uncircumcised, and with those who go down to the pit.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There are the princes of the north, all of them, and all the Sidonians, who are gone down with the slain; in the terror which they caused by their might they are put to shame; and they lie uncircumcised with those who are slain by the sword, and bear their shame with those who go down to the pit.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pharaoh shall see them, and shall be comforted over all his multitude, even Pharaoh and all his army, slain by the sword, says the Lord Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For I have put his terror in the land of the living; and he shall be laid among the uncircumcised, with those who are slain by the sword, even Pharaoh and all his multitude, says the Lord Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Son of man, speak to the children of your people, and tell them, When I bring the sword on a land, and the people of the land take a man from among them, and set him for their watchman;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: if, when he sees the sword come on the land, he blow the trumpet, and warn the people;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: then whoever hears the sound of the trumpet, and doesn’t take warning, if the sword come, and take him away, his blood shall be on his own head.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He heard the sound of the trumpet, and didn’t take warning; his blood shall be on him; whereas if he had taken warning, he would have delivered his soul.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But if the watchman sees the sword come, and doesn’t blow the trumpet, and the people aren’t warned, and the sword comes, and take any person from among them; he is taken away in his iniquity, but his blood will I require at the watchman’s hand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So you, son of man, I have set you a watchman to the house of Israel; therefore hear the word at my mouth, and give them warning from me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When I tell the wicked, O wicked man, you shall surely die, and you don’t speak to warn the wicked from his way; that wicked man shall die in his iniquity, but his blood will I require at your hand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Nevertheless, if you warn the wicked of his way to turn from it, and he doesn’t turn from his way; he shall die in his iniquity, but you have delivered your soul.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You, son of man, tell the house of Israel: Thus you speak, saying, Our transgressions and our sins are on us, and we pine away in them; how then can we live?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Tell them, As I live, says the Lord Yahweh, I have no pleasure in the death of the wicked; but that the wicked turn from his way and live: turn, turn from your evil ways; for why will you die, house of Israel?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You, son of man, tell the children of your people, The righteousness of the righteous shall not deliver him in the day of his disobedience; and as for the wickedness of the wicked, he shall not fall thereby in the day that he turns from his wickedness; neither shall he who is righteous be able to live thereby in the day that he sins.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When I tell the righteous, that he shall surely live; if he trust to his righteousness, and commit iniquity, none of his righteous deeds shall be remembered; but in his iniquity that he has committed, therein shall he die.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Again, when I say to the wicked, You shall surely die; if he turn from his sin, and do that which is lawful and right;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: if the wicked restore the pledge, give again that which he had taken by robbery, walk in the statutes of life, committing no iniquity; he shall surely live, he shall not die.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: None of his sins that he has committed shall be remembered against him: he has done that which is lawful and right; he shall surely live.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yet the children of your people say, The way of the Lord is not equal: but as for them, their way is not equal.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the righteous turns from his righteousness, and commits iniquity, he shall even die therein.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the wicked turns from his wickedness, and does that which is lawful and right, he shall live thereby.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yet you say, The way of the Lord is not equal. House of Israel, I will judge every one of you after his ways.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the twelfth year of our captivity, in the tenth month, in the fifth day of the month, one who had escaped out of Jerusalem came to me, saying, The city has been struck.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now Yahweh’s hand had been on me in the evening, before he who was escaped came; and he had opened my mouth, until he came to me in the morning; and my mouth was opened, and I was no more mute.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Son of man, they who inhabit those waste places in the land of Israel speak, saying, Abraham was one, and he inherited the land: but we are many; the land is given us for inheritance.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore tell them, Thus says the Lord Yahweh: You eat with the blood, and lift up your eyes to your idols, and shed blood: and shall you possess the land?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You stand on your sword, you work abomination, and every one of you defiles his neighbor’s wife: and shall you possess the land?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall tell them, Thus says the Lord Yahweh: As I live, surely those who are in the waste places shall fall by the sword; and him who is in the open field will I give to the animals to be devoured; and those who are in the strongholds and in the caves shall die of the pestilence.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will make the land a desolation and an astonishment; and the pride of her power shall cease; and the mountains of Israel shall be desolate, so that no one shall pass through.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then shall they know that I am Yahweh, when I have made the land a desolation and an astonishment, because of all their abominations which they have committed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As for you, son of man, the children of your people talk of you by the walls and in the doors of the houses, and speak to one another, everyone to his brother, saying, Please come and hear what is the word that comes out from Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They come to you as the people come, and they sit before you as my people, and they hear your words, but don’t do them; for with their mouth they show much love, but their heart goes after their gain.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, you are to them as a very lovely song of one who has a pleasant voice, and can play well on an instrument; for they hear your words, but they don’t do them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When this comes to pass, (behold, it comes), then shall they know that a prophet has been among them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Son of man, prophesy against the shepherds of Israel, prophesy, and tell them, even to the shepherds, Thus says the Lord Yahweh: Woe to the shepherds of Israel who feed themselves! Shouldn’t the shepherds feed the sheep?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You eat the fat, and you clothe yourself with the wool, you kill the fatlings; but you don’t feed the sheep.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You haven’t strengthened the diseased, neither have you healed that which was sick, neither have you bound up that which was broken, neither have you brought back that which was driven away, neither have you sought that which was lost; but with force and with rigor you have ruled over them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They were scattered, because there was no shepherd; and they became food to all the animals of the field, and were scattered.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My sheep wandered through all the mountains, and on every high hill: yes, my sheep were scattered on all the surface of the earth; and there was no one who searched or sought.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore, you shepherds, hear Yahweh’s word:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As I live, says the Lord Yahweh, surely because my sheep became a prey, and my sheep became food to all the animals of the field, because there was no shepherd, neither did my shepherds search for my sheep, but the shepherds fed themselves, and didn’t feed my sheep;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: therefore, you shepherds, hear Yahweh’s word:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thus says the Lord Yahweh: Behold, I am against the shepherds; and I will require my sheep at their hand, and cause them to cease from feeding the sheep; neither shall the shepherds feed themselves any more; and I will deliver my sheep from their mouth, that they may not be food for them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For thus says the Lord Yahweh: Behold, I myself, even I, will search for my sheep, and will seek them out.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As a shepherd seeks out his flock in the day that he is among his sheep that are scattered abroad, so will I seek out my sheep; and I will deliver them out of all places where they have been scattered in the cloudy and dark day.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will bring them out from the peoples, and gather them from the countries, and will bring them into their own land; and I will feed them on the mountains of Israel, by the watercourses, and in all the inhabited places of the country.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will feed them with good pasture; and on the mountains of the height of Israel shall their fold be: there shall they lie down in a good fold; and on fat pasture shall they feed on the mountains of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I myself will be the shepherd of my sheep, and I will cause them to lie down, says the Lord Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will seek that which was lost, and will bring back that which was driven away, and will bind up that which was broken, and will strengthen that which was sick: but the fat and the strong I will destroy; I will feed them in justice.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As for you, O my flock, thus says the Lord Yahweh: Behold, I judge between sheep and sheep, the rams and the male goats.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Does it seem a small thing to you to have fed on the good pasture, but you must tread down with your feet the residue of your pasture? and to have drunk of the clear waters, but you must foul the residue with your feet?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As for my sheep, they eat that which you have trodden with your feet, and they drink that which you have fouled with your feet.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore thus says the Lord Yahweh to them: Behold, I, even I, will judge between the fat sheep and the lean sheep.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Because you thrust with side and with shoulder, and push all the diseased with your horns, until you have scattered them abroad;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: therefore will I save my flock, and they shall no more be a prey; and I will judge between sheep and sheep.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will set up one shepherd over them, and he shall feed them, even my servant David; he shall feed them, and he shall be their shepherd.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I, Yahweh, will be their God, and my servant David prince among them; I, Yahweh, have spoken it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will make with them a covenant of peace, and will cause evil animals to cease out of the land; and they shall dwell securely in the wilderness, and sleep in the woods.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will make them and the places around my hill a blessing; and I will cause the shower to come down in its season; there shall be showers of blessing.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The tree of the field shall yield its fruit, and the earth shall yield its increase, and they shall be secure in their land; and they shall know that I am Yahweh, when I have broken the bars of their yoke, and have delivered them out of the hand of those who made slaves of them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They shall no more be a prey to the nations, neither shall the animals of the earth devour them; but they shall dwell securely, and no one shall make them afraid.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will raise up to them a plantation for renown, and they shall be no more consumed with famine in the land, neither bear the shame of the nations any more.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They shall know that I, Yahweh, their God am with them, and that they, the house of Israel, are my people, says the Lord Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You my sheep, the sheep of my pasture, are men, and I am your God, says the Lord Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Son of man, set your face against Mount Seir, and prophesy against it,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and tell it, Thus says the Lord Yahweh: Behold, I am against you, Mount Seir, and I will stretch out my hand against you, and I will make you a desolation and an astonishment.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will lay your cities waste, and you shall be desolate; and you shall know that I am Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Because you have had a perpetual hostility, and have given over the children of Israel to the power of the sword in the time of their calamity, in the time of the iniquity of the end;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: therefore, as I live, says the Lord Yahweh, I will prepare you for blood, and blood shall pursue you: since you have not hated blood, therefore blood shall pursue you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thus will I make Mount Seir an astonishment and a desolation; and I will cut off from it him who passes through and him who returns.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will fill its mountains with its slain: in your hills and in your valleys and in all your watercourses shall they fall who are slain with the sword.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will make you a perpetual desolation, and your cities shall not be inhabited; and you shall know that I am Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Because you have said, These two nations and these two countries shall be mine, and we will possess it; whereas Yahweh was there:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: therefore, as I live, says the Lord Yahweh, I will do according to your anger, and according to your envy which you have shown out of your hatred against them; and I will make myself known among them, when I shall judge you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall know that I, Yahweh, have heard all your insults which you have spoken against the mountains of Israel, saying, They are laid desolate, they have been given us to devour.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You have magnified yourselves against me with your mouth, and have multiplied your words against me: I have heard it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thus says the Lord Yahweh: When the whole earth rejoices, I will make you desolate.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As you rejoiced over the inheritance of the house of Israel, because it was desolate, so will I do to you: you shall be desolate, Mount Seir, and all Edom, even all of it; and they shall know that I am Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You, son of man, prophesy to the mountains of Israel, and say, You mountains of Israel, hear Yahweh’s word.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thus says the Lord Yahweh: Because the enemy has said against you, Aha! and, The ancient high places are ours in possession;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: therefore prophesy, and say, Thus says the Lord Yahweh: Because, even because they have made you desolate, and swallowed you up on every side, that you might be a possession to the residue of the nations, and you are taken up in the lips of talkers, and the evil report of the people;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: therefore, you mountains of Israel, hear the word of the Lord Yahweh: Thus says the Lord Yahweh to the mountains and to the hills, to the watercourses and to the valleys, to the desolate wastes and to the cities that are forsaken, which are become a prey and derision to the residue of the nations that are all around;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: therefore thus says the Lord Yahweh: Surely in the fire of my jealousy I have spoken against the residue of the nations, and against all Edom, that have appointed my land to themselves for a possession with the joy of all their heart, with despite of soul, to cast it out for a prey.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore prophesy concerning the land of Israel, and tell the mountains and to the hills, to the watercourses and to the valleys, Thus says the Lord Yahweh: Behold, I have spoken in my jealousy and in my wrath, because you have borne the shame of the nations:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: therefore thus says the Lord Yahweh: I have sworn, Surely the nations that are around you, they shall bear their shame.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But you, mountains of Israel, you shall shoot out your branches, and yield your fruit to my people Israel; for they are at hand to come.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For, behold, I am for you, and I will turn into you, and you shall be tilled and sown;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and I will multiply men on you, all the house of Israel, even all of it; and the cities shall be inhabited, and the waste places shall be built;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and I will multiply on you man and animal; and they shall increase and be fruitful; and I will cause you to be inhabited after your former estate, and you will do better than at your beginnings: and you shall know that I am Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yes, I will cause men to walk on you, even my people Israel; and they shall possess you, and you shall be their inheritance, and you shall no more henceforth bereave them of children.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thus says the Lord Yahweh: Because they say to you, You are a devourer of men, and have been a bereaver of your nation;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: therefore you shall devour men no more, neither bereave your nation any more, says the Lord Yahweh;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: neither will I let you hear any more the shame of the nations, neither shall you bear the reproach of the peoples any more, neither shall you cause your nation to stumble any more, says the Lord Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Son of man, when the house of Israel lived in their own land, they defiled it by their way and by their doings: their way before me was as the uncleanness of a woman in her impurity.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore I poured out my wrath on them for the blood which they had poured out on the land, and because they had defiled it with their idols;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and I scattered them among the nations, and they were dispersed through the countries: according to their way and according to their doings I judged them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they came to the nations, where they went, they profaned my holy name; in that men said of them, These are the people of Yahweh, and are gone out of his land.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But I had respect for my holy name, which the house of Israel had profaned among the nations, where they went.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore tell the house of Israel, Thus says the Lord Yahweh: I don’t do this for your sake, house of Israel, but for my holy name, which you have profaned among the nations, where you went.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will sanctify my great name, which has been profaned among the nations, which you have profaned among them; and the nations shall know that I am Yahweh, says the Lord Yahweh, when I shall be sanctified in you before their eyes.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For I will take you from among the nations, and gather you out of all the countries, and will bring you into your own land.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will sprinkle clean water on you, and you shall be clean: from all your filthiness, and from all your idols, will I cleanse you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will also give you a new heart, and I will put a new spirit within you; and I will take away the stony heart out of your flesh, and I will give you a heart of flesh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will put my Spirit within you, and cause you to walk in my statutes, and you shall keep my ordinances, and do them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall dwell in the land that I gave to your fathers; and you shall be my people, and I will be your God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will save you from all your uncleanness: and I will call for the grain, and will multiply it, and lay no famine on you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will multiply the fruit of the tree, and the increase of the field, that you may receive no more the reproach of famine among the nations.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then you shall remember your evil ways, and your doings that were not good; and you shall loathe yourselves in your own sight for your iniquities and for your abominations.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Nor for your sake do I this, says the Lord Yahweh, be it known to you: be ashamed and confounded for your ways, house of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thus says the Lord Yahweh: In the day that I cleanse you from all your iniquities, I will cause the cities to be inhabited, and the waste places shall be built.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The land that was desolate shall be tilled, whereas it was a desolation in the sight of all who passed by.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They shall say, This land that was desolate has become like the garden of Eden; and the waste and desolate and ruined cities are fortified and inhabited.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then the nations that are left around you shall know that I, Yahweh, have built the ruined places, and planted that which was desolate: I, Yahweh, have spoken it, and I will do it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thus says the Lord Yahweh: For this, moreover, will I be inquired of by the house of Israel, to do it for them: I will increase them with men like a flock.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As the flock for sacrifice, as the flock of Jerusalem in her appointed feasts, so shall the waste cities be filled with flocks of men; and they shall know that I am Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh’s hand was on me, and he brought me out in Yahweh’s Spirit, and set me down in the middle of the valley; and it was full of bones.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He caused me to pass by them all around: and behold, there were very many in the open valley; and behold, they were very dry.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to me, Son of man, can these bones live? I answered, Lord Yahweh, you know.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Again he said to me, Prophesy over these bones, and tell them, you dry bones, hear Yahweh’s word.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thus says the Lord Yahweh to these bones: Behold, I will cause breath to enter into you, and you shall live.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will lay sinews on you, and will bring up flesh on you, and cover you with skin, and put breath in you, and you shall live; and you shall know that I am Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So I prophesied as I was commanded: and as I prophesied, there was a noise, and behold, an earthquake; and the bones came together, bone to its bone.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I saw, and, behold, there were sinews on them, and flesh came up, and skin covered them above; but there was no breath in them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then he said to me, Prophesy to the wind, prophesy, son of man, and tell the wind, Thus says the Lord Yahweh: Come from the four winds, breath, and breathe on these slain, that they may live.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So I prophesied as he commanded me, and the breath came into them, and they lived, and stood up on their feet, an exceedingly great army.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then he said to me, Son of man, these bones are the whole house of Israel: behold, they say, Our bones are dried up, and our hope is lost; we are clean cut off.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore prophesy, and tell them, Thus says the Lord Yahweh: Behold, I will open your graves, and cause you to come up out of your graves, my people; and I will bring you into the land of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall know that I am Yahweh, when I have opened your graves, and caused you to come up out of your graves, my people.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will put my Spirit in you, and you shall live, and I will place you in your own land: and you shall know that I, Yahweh, have spoken it and performed it, says Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh’s word came again to me, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You, son of man, take one stick, and write on it, For Judah, and for the children of Israel his companions: then take another stick, and write on it, For Joseph, the stick of Ephraim, and for all the house of Israel his companions:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and join them for you to one another into one stick, that they may become one in your hand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the children of your people shall speak to you, saying, Will you not show us what you mean by these?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: tell them, Thus says the Lord Yahweh: Behold, I will take the stick of Joseph, which is in the hand of Ephraim, and the tribes of Israel his companions; and I will put them with it, with the stick of Judah, and make them one stick, and they shall be one in my hand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The sticks whereon you write shall be in your hand before their eyes.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Say to them, Thus says the Lord Yahweh: Behold, I will take the children of Israel from among the nations, where they are gone, and will gather them on every side, and bring them into their own land:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and I will make them one nation in the land, on the mountains of Israel; and one king shall be king to them all; and they shall be no more two nations, neither shall they be divided into two kingdoms any more at all;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: neither shall they defile themselves any more with their idols, nor with their detestable things, nor with any of their transgressions; but I will save them out of all their dwelling places, in which they have sinned, and will cleanse them: so shall they be my people, and I will be their God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My servant David shall be king over them; and they all shall have one shepherd: they shall also walk in my ordinances, and observe my statutes, and do them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They shall dwell in the land that I have given to Jacob my servant, in which your fathers lived; and they shall dwell therein, they, and their children, and their children’s children, forever: and David my servant shall be their prince for ever.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover I will make a covenant of peace with them; it shall be an everlasting covenant with them; and I will place them, and multiply them, and will set my sanctuary among them forever more.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My tent also shall be with them; and I will be their God, and they shall be my people.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The nations shall know that I am Yahweh who sanctifies Israel, when my sanctuary shall be among them forever more.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Son of man, set your face toward Gog, of the land of Magog, the prince of Rosh, Meshech, and Tubal, and prophesy against him,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and say, Thus says the Lord Yahweh: Behold, I am against you, Gog, prince of Rosh, Meshech, and Tubal:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and I will turn you around, and put hooks into your jaws, and I will bring you out, with all your army, horses and horsemen, all of them clothed in full armor, a great company with buckler and shield, all of them handling swords;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Persia, Cush, and Put with them, all of them with shield and helmet;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Gomer, and all his hordes; the house of Togarmah in the uttermost parts of the north, and all his hordes; even many peoples with you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Be prepared, yes, prepare yourself, you, and all your companies who are assembled to you, and be a guard to them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: After many days you shall be visited: in the latter years you shall come into the land that is brought back from the sword, that is gathered out of many peoples, on the mountains of Israel, which have been a continual waste; but it is brought out of the peoples, and they shall dwell securely, all of them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall ascend, you shall come like a storm, you shall be like a cloud to cover the land, you, and all your hordes, and many peoples with you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thus says the Lord Yahweh: It shall happen in that day, that things shall come into your mind, and you shall devise an evil device:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and you shall say, I will go up to the land of unwalled villages; I will go to those who are at rest, who dwell securely, all of them dwelling without walls, and having neither bars nor gates;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: to take the plunder and to take the prey; to turn your hand against the waste places that are inhabited, and against the people who are gathered out of the nations, who have gotten livestock and goods, who dwell in the middle of the earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Sheba, and Dedan, and the merchants of Tarshish, with all the young lions of it, shall tell you, Have you come to take the plunder? have you assembled your company to take the prey? to carry away silver and gold, to take away livestock and goods, to take great plunder?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore, son of man, prophesy, and tell Gog, Thus says the Lord Yahweh: In that day when my people Israel dwells securely, shall you not know it?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall come from your place out of the uttermost parts of the north, you, and many peoples with you, all of them riding on horses, a great company and a mighty army;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and you shall come up against my people Israel, as a cloud to cover the land: it shall happen in the latter days, that I will bring you against my land, that the nations may know me, when I shall be sanctified in you, Gog, before their eyes.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thus says the Lord Yahweh: Are you he of whom I spoke in old time by my servants the prophets of Israel, who prophesied in those days for years that I would bring you against them?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It shall happen in that day, when Gog shall come against the land of Israel, says the Lord Yahweh, that my wrath shall come up into my nostrils.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For in my jealousy and in the fire of my wrath I have spoken, Surely in that day there shall be a great shaking in the land of Israel;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: so that the fish of the sea, and the birds of the sky, and the animals of the field, and all creeping things who creep on the earth, and all the men who are on the surface of the earth, shall shake at my presence, and the mountains shall be thrown down, and the steep places shall fall, and every wall shall fall to the ground.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will call for a sword against him to all my mountains, says the Lord Yahweh: every man’s sword shall be against his brother.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: With pestilence and with blood will I enter into judgment with him; and I will rain on him, and on his hordes, and on the many peoples who are with him, an overflowing shower, and great hailstones, fire, and sulfur.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will magnify myself, and sanctify myself, and I will make myself known in the eyes of many nations; and they shall know that I am Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You, son of man, prophesy against Gog, and say, Thus says the Lord Yahweh: Behold, I am against you, Gog, prince of Rosh, Meshech, and Tubal:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and I will turn you around, and will lead you on, and will cause you to come up from the uttermost parts of the north; and I will bring you on the mountains of Israel;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and I will strike your bow out of your left hand, and will cause your arrows to fall out of your right hand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall fall on the mountains of Israel, you, and all your hordes, and the peoples who are with you: I will give you to the ravenous birds of every sort, and to the animals of the field to be devoured.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall fall on the open field; for I have spoken it, says the Lord Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will send a fire on Magog, and on those who dwell securely in the islands; and they shall know that I am Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My holy name will I make known among my people Israel; neither will I allow my holy name to be profaned any more: and the nations shall know that I am Yahweh, the Holy One in Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, it comes, and it shall be done, says the Lord Yahweh; this is the day about which I have spoken.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Those who dwell in the cities of Israel shall go out, and shall make fires of the weapons and burn them, both the shields and the bucklers, the bows and the arrows, and the war clubs, and the spears, and they shall make fires of them seven years;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: so that they shall take no wood out of the field, neither cut down any out of the forests; for they shall make fires of the weapons; and they shall plunder those who plundered them, and rob those who robbed them, says the Lord Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It shall happen in that day, that I will give to Gog a place for burial in Israel, the valley of those who pass through on the east of the sea; and it shall stop those who pass through: and there shall they bury Gog and all his multitude; and they shall call it The valley of Hamon Gog.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Seven months shall the house of Israel be burying them, that they may cleanse the land.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yes, all the people of the land shall bury them; and it shall be to them a renown in the day that I shall be glorified, says the Lord Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They shall set apart men of continual employment, who shall pass through the land, and, with those who pass through, those who bury those who remain on the surface of the land, to cleanse it: after the end of seven months shall they search.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Those who pass through the land shall pass through; and when any sees a man’s bone, then shall he set up a sign by it, until the undertakers have buried it in the valley of Hamon Gog.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Hamonah shall also be the name of a city. Thus shall they cleanse the land.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You, son of man, thus says the Lord Yahweh: Speak to the birds of every sort, and to every animal of the field, Assemble yourselves, and come; gather yourselves on every side to my sacrifice that I do sacrifice for you, even a great sacrifice on the mountains of Israel, that you may eat meat and drink blood.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall eat the flesh of the mighty, and drink the blood of the princes of the earth, of rams, of lambs, and of goats, of bulls, all of them fatlings of Bashan.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall eat fat until you be full, and drink blood until you are drunk, of my sacrifice which I have sacrificed for you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall be filled at my table with horses and chariots, with mighty men, and with all men of war, says the Lord Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will set my glory among the nations; and all the nations shall see my judgment that I have executed, and my hand that I have laid on them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So the house of Israel shall know that I am Yahweh their God, from that day and forward.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The nations shall know that the house of Israel went into captivity for their iniquity; because they trespassed against me, and I hid my face from them: so I gave them into the hand of their adversaries, and they fell all of them by the sword.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: According to their uncleanness and according to their transgressions did I to them; and I hid my face from them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore thus says the Lord Yahweh: Now will I bring back the captivity of Jacob, and have mercy on the whole house of Israel; and I will be jealous for my holy name.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They shall bear their shame, and all their trespasses by which they have trespassed against me, when they shall dwell securely in their land, and no one shall make them afraid;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: when I have brought them back from the peoples, and gathered them out of their enemies’ lands, and am sanctified in them in the sight of many nations.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They shall know that I am Yahweh their God, in that I caused them to go into captivity among the nations, and have gathered them to their own land; and I will leave none of them any more there;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: neither will I hide my face any more from them; for I have poured out my Spirit on the house of Israel, says the Lord Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the twenty-fifth year of our captivity, in the beginning of the year, in the tenth day of the month, in the fourteenth year after that the city was struck, in the same day, Yahweh’s hand was on me, and he brought me there.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the visions of God brought he me into the land of Israel, and set me down on a very high mountain, whereon was as it were the frame of a city on the south.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He brought me there; and, behold, there was a man, whose appearance was like the appearance of brass, with a line of flax in his hand, and a measuring reed; and he stood in the gate.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The man said to me, Son of man, see with your eyes, and hear with your ears, and set your heart on all that I shall show you; for, to the intent that I may show them to you, you are brought here: declare all that you see to the house of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, a wall on the outside of the house all around, and in the man’s hand a measuring reed six cubits  long, of a cubit and a hand width each: so he measured the thickness of the building, one reed; and the height, one reed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then came he to the gate which looks toward the east, and went up its steps: and he measured the threshold of the gate, one reed wide; and the other threshold, one reed wide.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Every lodge was one reed long, and one reed wide; and between the lodges was five cubits; and the threshold of the gate by the porch of the gate toward the house was one reed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He measured also the porch of the gate toward the house, one reed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then measured he the porch of the gate, eight cubits; and its posts, two cubits; and the porch of the gate was toward the house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The lodges of the gate eastward were three on this side, and three on that side; they three were of one measure: and the posts had one measure on this side and on that side.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He measured the width of the opening of the gate, ten cubits; and the length of the gate, thirteen cubits;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and a border before the lodges, one cubit on this side, and a border, one cubit on that side; and the lodges, six cubits on this side, and six cubits on that side.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He measured the gate from the roof of the one lodge to the roof of the other, a width of twenty-five cubits; door against door.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made also posts, sixty cubits; and the court reached to the posts, around the gate.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: From the forefront of the gate at the entrance to the forefront of the inner porch of the gate were fifty cubits.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There were closed windows to the lodges, and to their posts within the gate all around, and likewise to the arches; and windows were around inward; and on each post were palm trees.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then brought he me into the outer court; and behold, there were rooms and a pavement, made for the court all around: thirty rooms were on the pavement.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The pavement was by the side of the gates, answerable to the length of the gates, even the lower pavement.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then he measured the width from the forefront of the lower gate to the forefront of the inner court outside, one hundred cubits, both on the east and on the north.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The gate of the outer court whose prospect is toward the north, he measured its length and its width.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The lodges of it were three on this side and three on that side; and its posts and its arches were after the measure of the first gate: its length was fifty cubits, and the width twenty-five cubits.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The windows of it, and its arches, and the palm trees of it, were after the measure of the gate whose prospect is toward the east; and they went up to it by seven steps; and its arches were before them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There was a gate to the inner court facing the other gate, on the north and on the east; and he measured from gate to gate one hundred cubits.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He led me toward the south; and behold, a gate toward the south: and he measured its posts and its arches according to these measurements.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There were windows in it and in its arches all around, like those windows: the length was fifty cubits, and the width twenty-five cubits.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There were seven steps to go up to it, and its arches were before them; and it had palm trees, one on this side, and another on that side, on its posts.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There was a gate to the inner court toward the south: and he measured from gate to gate toward the south a hundred cubits.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then he brought me to the inner court by the south gate: and he measured the south gate according to these measurements;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and its lodges, and its posts, and its arches, according to these measurements: and there were windows in it and in its arches all around; it was fifty cubits long, and twenty-five cubits wide.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There were arches all around, twenty-five cubits long, and five cubits wide.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The arches of it were toward the outer court; and palm trees were on its posts: and the ascent to it had eight steps.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He brought me into the inner court toward the east: and he measured the gate according to these measurements;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and its lodges, and its posts, and its arches, according to these measurements: and there were windows therein and in its arches all around; it was fifty cubits long, and twenty-five cubits wide.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The arches of it were toward the outer court; and palm trees were on its posts, on this side, and on that side: and the ascent to it had eight steps.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He brought me to the north gate: and he measured it according to these measurements;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: its lodges, its posts, and its arches: and there were windows therein all around; the length was fifty cubits, and the width twenty-five cubits.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The posts of it were toward the outer court; and palm trees were on its posts, on this side, and on that side: and the ascent to it had eight steps.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A room with its door was by the posts at the gates; there they washed the burnt offering.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the porch of the gate were two tables on this side, and two tables on that side, on which to kill the burnt offering, the sin offering, and the trespass offering.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: On the one side outside, as one goes up to the entry of the gate toward the north, were two tables; and on the other side, which belonged to the porch of the gate, were two tables.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Four tables were on this side, and four tables on that side, by the side of the gate; eight tables, whereupon they killed the sacrifices.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There were four tables for the burnt offering, of cut stone, a cubit and a half long, and a cubit and a half wide, and one cubit high; whereupon they laid the instruments with which they killed the burnt offering and the sacrifice.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The hooks, a hand width long, were fastened within all around: and on the tables was the meat of the offering.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Outside of the inner gate were rooms for the singers in the inner court, which was at the side of the north gate; and their prospect was toward the south; one at the side of the east gate having the prospect toward the north.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to me, This room, whose prospect is toward the south, is for the priests, the keepers of the duty of the house;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and the room whose prospect is toward the north is for the priests, the keepers of the duty of the altar: these are the sons of Zadok, who from among the sons of Levi come near to Yahweh to minister to him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He measured the court, one hundred cubits long, and a hundred cubits wide, square; and the altar was before the house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then he brought me to the porch of the house, and measured each post of the porch, five cubits on this side, and five cubits on that side: and the width of the gate was three cubits on this side, and three cubits on that side.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The length of the porch was twenty cubits, and the width eleven cubits; even by the steps by which they went up to it: and there were pillars by the posts, one on this side, and another on that side.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He brought me to the temple, and measured the posts, six cubits wide on the one side, and six cubits wide on the other side, which was the width of the tent.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The width of the entrance was ten cubits;  and the sides of the entrance were five cubits on the one side, and five cubits on the other side: and he measured its length, forty cubits, and the width, twenty cubits.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then went he inward, and measured each post of the entrance, two cubits; and the entrance, six cubits; and the width of the entrance, seven cubits.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He measured its length, twenty cubits, and the width, twenty cubits, before the temple: and he said to me, This is the most holy place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then he measured the wall of the house, six cubits; and the width of every side room, four cubits, all around the house on every side.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The side rooms were in three stories, one over another, and thirty in order; and they entered into the wall which belonged to the house for the side rooms all around, that they might be supported, and not penetrate the wall of the house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The side rooms were wider on the higher levels, because the walls were narrower at the higher levels. Therefore the width of the house increased upward; and so one went up from the lowest level to the highest through the middle level.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I saw also that the house had a raised base all around: the foundations of the side rooms were a full reed of six great cubits.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The thickness of the wall, which was for the side rooms, on the outside, was five cubits: and that which was left was the place of the side rooms that belonged to the house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Between the rooms was a width of twenty cubits around the house on every side.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The doors of the side rooms were toward an open area that was left, one door toward the north, and another door toward the south. The width of the open area was five cubits all around.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The building that was before the separate place at the side toward the west was seventy cubits wide; and the wall of the building was five cubits thick all around, and its length ninety cubits.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So he measured the house, one hundred cubits long; and the separate place, and the building, with its walls, one hundred cubits long;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: also the width of the face of the house, and of the separate place toward the east, one hundred cubits.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He measured the length of the building before the separate place which was at its back, and its galleries on the one side and on the other side, one hundred cubits; and the inner temple, and the porches of the court;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the thresholds, and the closed windows, and the galleries around on their three stories, over against the threshold, with wood ceilings all around, and from the ground up to the windows, (now the windows were covered),

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: to the space above the door, even to the inner house, and outside, and by all the wall all around inside and outside, by measure.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It was made with cherubim and palm trees; and a palm tree was between cherub and cherub, and every cherub had two faces;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: so that there was the face of a man toward the palm tree on the one side, and the face of a young lion toward the palm tree on the other side. It was made like this through all the house all around:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: from the ground to above the door were cherubim and palm trees made: thus was the wall of the temple.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As for the temple, the door posts were squared; and as for the face of the sanctuary, its appearance was as the appearance of the temple.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The altar was of wood, three cubits high, and its length two cubits; and its corners, and its length, and its walls, were of wood: and he said to me, This is the table that is before Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The temple and the sanctuary had two doors.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The doors had two leaves each, two turning leaves: two for the one door, and two leaves for the other.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There were made on them, on the doors of the temple, cherubim and palm trees, like as were made on the walls; and there was a threshold of wood on the face of the porch outside.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There were closed windows and palm trees on the one side and on the other side, on the sides of the porch: thus were the side rooms of the house, and the thresholds.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then he brought me out into the outer court, the way toward the north: and he brought me into the room that was over against the separate place, and which was over against the building toward the north.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Before the length of one hundred cubits  was the north door, and the width was fifty cubits.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Over against the twenty cubits which belonged to the inner court, and over against the pavement which belonged to the outer court, was gallery against gallery in the third floor.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Before the rooms was a walk of ten cubits’ width inward, a way of one cubit; and their doors were toward the north.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now the upper rooms were shorter; for the galleries took away from these, more than from the lower and the middle, in the building.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For they were in three stories, and they didn’t have pillars as the pillars of the courts: therefore the uppermost was set back more than the lowest and the middle from the ground.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The wall that was outside by the side of the rooms, toward the outer court before the rooms, its length was fifty cubits.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the length of the rooms that were in the outer court was fifty cubits: and behold, before the temple were one hundred cubits.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: From under these rooms was the entry on the east side, as one goes into them from the outer court.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the thickness of the wall of the court toward the east, before the separate place, and before the building, there were rooms.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The way before them was like the appearance of the rooms which were toward the north; according to their length so was their width: and all their exits were both according to their fashions, and according to their doors.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: According to the doors of the rooms that were toward the south was a door at the head of the way, even the way directly before the wall toward the east, as one enters into them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then he said to me, The north rooms and the south rooms, which are before the separate place, they are the holy rooms, where the priests who are near to Yahweh shall eat the most holy things: there shall they lay the most holy things, and the meal offering, and the sin offering, and the trespass offering; for the place is holy.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the priests enter in, then shall they not go out of the holy place into the outer court, but there they shall lay their garments in which they minister; for they are holy: and they shall put on other garments, and shall approach to that which is for the people.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now when he had finished measuring the inner house, he brought me out by the way of the gate whose prospect is toward the east, and measured it all around.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He measured on the east side with the measuring reed five hundred reeds, with the measuring reed all around.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He measured on the north side five hundred reeds with the measuring reed all around.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He measured on the south side five hundred reeds with the measuring reed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He turned about to the west side, and measured five hundred reeds with the measuring reed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He measured it on the four sides: it had a wall around it, the length five hundred, and the width five hundred, to make a separation between that which was holy and that which was common.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Afterward he brought me to the gate, even the gate that looks toward the east.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, the glory of the God of Israel came from the way of the east: and his voice was like the sound of many waters; and the earth shined with his glory.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It was according to the appearance of the vision which I saw, even according to the vision that I saw when I came to destroy the city; and the visions were like the vision that I saw by the river Chebar; and I fell on my face.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh’s glory came into the house by the way of the gate whose prospect is toward the east.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Spirit took me up, and brought me into the inner court; and behold, Yahweh’s glory filled the house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I heard one speaking to me out of the house; and a man stood by me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to me, Son of man, this is the place of my throne, and the place of the soles of my feet, where I will dwell among the children of Israel forever. The house of Israel shall no more defile my holy name, neither they, nor their kings, by their prostitution, and by the dead bodies of their kings in their high places;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: in their setting of their threshold by my threshold, and their door post beside my door post, and there was a wall between me and them; and they have defiled my holy name by their abominations which they have committed: therefore I have consumed them in my anger.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now let them put away their prostitution, and the dead bodies of their kings, far from me; and I will dwell among them forever.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You, son of man, show the house to the house of Israel, that they may be ashamed of their iniquities; and let them measure the pattern.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If they be ashamed of all that they have done, make known to them the form of the house, and its fashion, and its exits, and its entrances, and all its forms, and all its ordinances, and all its forms, and all its laws; and write it in their sight; that they may keep the whole form of it, and all its ordinances, and do them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: This is the law of the house: on the top of the mountain the whole limit around it shall be most holy. Behold, this is the law of the house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: These are the measurements of the altar by cubits (the cubit  is a cubit and a hand width): the bottom shall be a cubit, and the width a cubit, and its border around its edge a span;  and this shall be the base of the altar.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: From the bottom on the ground to the lower ledge shall be two cubits, and the width one cubit; and from the lesser ledge to the greater ledge shall be four cubits, and the width a cubit.

translations.name: World English Bible
